title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot investigates a series of odd missing or damaged items at a dormitory. He reviews a list of items that seem unlikely to be stolen, including Mr. McNab's old flannel pants, light bulbs, borax, bath salt, and recipes. However, he identifies more suspicious items: a torn canvas backpack, which likely belongs to either Leonard Betson or Colin McNaughton, and a delicate green silk scarf belonging to Valerie Hobhouse that was cut up. He also notes the disappearance of one evening shoe belonging to Sally Finch, an American Fulbright scholar. Poirot suspects the destruction of the scarf and backpack was motivated by malice.

To investigate the shoe, Poirot instructs Miss Lemon to have Mrs. Hubbard report the loss to the lost property office at Baker Street Station or the Lambeth Police Station, suggesting she claim it was lost on a train or in a taxi. Poirot also pressures Mrs. Harbottle to provide information regarding the students' personal relationships, temperaments, and potential jealousies, suspecting she is protecting someone she likes.

Later, Mrs. Habberd returns to 26 Mountnut Street, where she is greeted by Leonard "Lennie" Bateson. Lennie makes gruesome jokes about "chopping up a lovely corpse" and mentions a reaction from a girl named Sylvia. He is joined by Nigel Chapman, a lanky, long-haired young man. Before Mrs. Habberd can settle in, she is informed that Mrs. Nicolaitis wishes to see her.
